summary: Spanish author, member of the Generation of '98. Known for the 'nivolas' Mist (Niebla) and Abel Sanchez, the novella San Manuel Bueno, Martír, and the philosophical essay The Tragic Sense of Life. Coined the term 'nivola.'

works.name: Mist (Niebla)
works.description: Metafictional 'nivola' in which Augusto Pérez confronts the author Unamuno himself.

clues.clue: Augusto confronts Unamuno (the author) who tells him he can't die because he's fictional; Augusto dies mysteriously anyway
examples: he inserted himself into one of his nivolas, in which he is confronted by his character Augusto

clues.clue: Funeral oration given by Augusto's dog Orfeo
examples: used a funeral oration given by the dog Orfeo to end an existential 1914 novel

clues.clue: Prologue by fictional author Victor Goti; post-prologue by Unamuno disagreeing
examples: includes a 'post-prologue' by the author that disagrees with the prologue written by the character Victor Goti

clues.clue: Eugenia jilts Augusto for Mauricio, leading him to contemplate suicide
examples: Eugenia's rejection of Augusto leads Augusto to attempt suicide

works.name: Abel Sanchez
works.description: Novel retelling the Cain and Abel story with doctor Joaquín and painter Abel.

clues.clue: Retelling of Cain and Abel: doctor Joaquín Monegro envies painter Abel Sanchez; Joaquín kills Abel by grabbing his throat
examples: wrote a novel inspired by the Cain and Abel story about Joaquin Monegro's jealousy of the title artist

clues.clue: Joaquín reads Lord Byron's Cain after Abel begins a painting; on deathbed, declares his wife Antonia couldn't cure his envy because he didn't love her
examples: the protagonist Joaquin is based on the story of this character

works.name: San Manuel Bueno, Martír
works.description: Novella about a priest who secretly doesn't believe in God.

clues.clue: Lazarus reveals to sister Angela that the title priest is a non-believer who uses religion to spread virtue; set in Valverde de Lucerna on a lake hiding a submerged city
examples: Lazarus exposes the title character's atheism to Angela
the title priest loses his faith but does not tell his parishioners

clues.clue: Don Manuel tells Lázaro 'Let us give them opium so that they can sleep and dream'
examples: tells his pupil Lazaro 'Let us give them opium so that they can sleep and dream'

works.name: The Tragic Sense of Life
works.description: Philosophical essay arguing that reason is the enemy of life.

clues.clue: Opens with 'the man of flesh and bone'; reformulates cogito ergo sum as sum ergo cogito; 'reason is the enemy of life'; chapter on Don Quixote
examples: reformulated Descartes's cogito, ergo sum as sum, ergo cogito
argues that 'reason is the enemy of life'

clues.clue: Member of Spain's Generation of '98; coined the term 'nivola'
examples: name this member of Spain's Generation of '98

clues.clue: Basque-born; wrote The Christ of Velazquez
examples: He wrote a poetic work called The Christ of Velazquez

comprehensive_summary:
Miguel de Unamuno (1864-1936) was a Spanish author and philosopher, a leading member of the Generation of '98. He coined the nonsense term 'nivola' to distinguish his novels from conventional realism.

Mist (Niebla) is a metafictional nivola in which Augusto Pérez, jilted by Eugenia, confronts Unamuno himself, who tells him he cannot commit suicide because he is a fictional character. Augusto dies mysteriously, and his dog Orfeo delivers the funeral oration. The novel includes a prologue by the fictional Victor Goti and a disagreeing post-prologue by Unamuno.

Abel Sanchez retells Cain and Abel: the doctor Joaquín Monegro envies the painter Abel Sanchez throughout their lives. Joaquín kills Abel by grabbing his throat, and on his deathbed declares his wife Antonia could not cure his envy because he didn't love her.

San Manuel Bueno, Martír is set in Valverde de Lucerna on a lake said to hide a submerged city. Lazarus reveals to his sister Angela that the beloved priest Don Manuel secretly doesn't believe in God. The Tragic Sense of Life opens with 'the man of flesh and bone' and reformulates Descartes's cogito as 'sum ergo cogito.'

cards.front: Novel: Augusto confronts the author himself, who tells him he can't die because he's fictional; Augusto's dog Orfeo delivers the funeral oration
Novel: retells Cain and Abel; doctor Joaquín Monegro envies painter Abel Sanchez and eventually kills him by grabbing his throat
Novel: Lazarus reveals to Angela that the title priest secretly doesn't believe in God; set in Valverde de Lucerna on a lake hiding a submerged city
cards.back: San Manuel Bueno, Martír (Miguel de Unamuno)

cards.front: Author: reformulated Descartes's cogito as 'sum ergo cogito'; essay opens with 'the man of flesh and bone'; argues 'reason is the enemy of life'
cards.back: The Tragic Sense of Life (Miguel de Unamuno)
